perturbation.original_text: 8.2.2 The Monthly Fees will be paid to us at the place and in the manner we designate on or before the fifteenth (15th) day of each month and will be accompanied by our standard schedule setting forth in reasonable detail the computation of the Monthly Fees for such month.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.2.2 The Monthly Fees will be paid to us at the place and in the manner we designate on or before the first (1st) day of each month and will be accompanied by our standard schedule setting forth in reasonable detail the computation of the Monthly Fees for such month.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by modifying the payment deadline for Monthly Fees. Originally, fees were due by the 15th of each month, but now they are due by the 1st. This conflicts directly with Subsection 10.1.1, which indirectly references the 15th as the deadline for submitting the Operational Information needed to calculate these fees. This inserted change will create uncertainty and disputes over when payments are actually due, potentially leading to late fee disputes and other contractual disagreements, because now 8.2.2 states the 1st, and Subsection 10.1.1 states the 15th.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.1.1 At our request, you will prepare and deliver to us the Reports containing the Operational Information (and any other information we reasonable require) in the form, manner and time frame we require. At a minimum, by the fifteenth (15th) day of each month, you will submit to us the Operational Information for the previous month and reflecting the computation of the amounts then due under Section 8, in the form, manner and time frame we require.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment due date from the 15th to the 1st of each month (8.2.2). This directly contradicts subsection 10.1.1, which states that the operational information needed to calculate the monthly fees must be submitted by the 15th of each month. Therefore, it is impossible to calculate the fees and make payment by the 1st, as required by the changed 8.2.2, when the information needed to calculate it, according to 10.1.1, is only submitted by the 15th. This introduces a clear inconsistency in the contract regarding payment terms and deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: 14.4.1 Calculation of Liquidated Damages. You acknowledge and agree that the premature termination of this Agreement will cause substantial damage to us. You agree that Liquidated Damages are not a penalty, but represent a reasonable estimate of the minimum just and fair compensation for the damages we will suffer as the result of your failure to operate the Hotel for the Term.
perturbation.changed_text: 14.4.1 Calculation of Liquidated Damages. You acknowledge and agree that the premature termination of this Agreement will not cause substantial damage to us. You agree that Liquidated Damages are a penalty, and do not represent a reasonable estimate of the minimum just and fair compensation for the damages we will suffer as the result of your failure to operate the Hotel for the Term.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by asserting that liquidated damages are a penalty and that premature termination of the agreement does not cause substantial damage. This directly contradicts the purpose and enforceability of the liquidated damages clause itself, stated in multiple subsections (14.4.1.1 to 14.4.1.5) where specific amounts are calculated based on the premise of damages suffered due to early termination. By claiming damages are non-existent, this renders the clause contradictory and unenforceable, creating uncertainty and disputes over the validity of these charges because it makes the mention of 'Liquidated Damages', no longer 'Liquidated'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14.4.1.1 If termination occurs before you begin the Hotel Work and before the Opening Date, and you or any Guarantor (or your or any Guarantor's Affiliates) directly or indirectly, enter into a franchise, license, management, lease and/or other similar agreement for or begin construction or commence operation of a hotel, motel, inn, or similar facility at the Hotel Site under a Competitor Brand within one (1) year after termination, then you will pay us Liquidated Damages in an amount equal to $3,600 multiplied by the number of approved Guest Rooms at the Hotel.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that premature termination will not cause substantial damage and that liquidated damages are a penalty, directly contradicting the contradicted text (14.4.1.1) which calculates liquidated damages based on a formula tied to the number of guest rooms, implying damages exist. This inconsistency regarding the nature of liquidated damages (penalty vs. compensation for damages) creates a strong in-text contradiction, making the liquidated damages clause unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 13.2 Your Transfer. You understand and acknowledge that the rights and duties in this Agreement are personal to you and that we are entering into this Agreement in reliance on your business skill, financial capacity, and the personal character of you, your officers, directors, partners, members, shareholders or trustees. A Transfer by you of any Equity Interest, or this Agreement, or any of your rights or obligations under this Agreement, or a Transfer by an Equity Owner is prohibited other than as expressly permitted herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.2 Your Transfer. You understand and acknowledge that the rights and duties in this Agreement are not personal to you and that we are not entering into this Agreement in reliance on your business skill, financial capacity, and the personal character of you, your officers, directors, partners, members, shareholders or trustees. A Transfer by you of any Equity Interest, or this Agreement, or any of your rights or obligations under this Agreement, or a Transfer by an Equity Owner is not prohibited and can be done as expressly permitted herein.
perturbation.explanation: This modification creates an in-text contradiction by negating the personal nature of the agreement and allowing transfers of equity interest, contradicting the subsequent detailed sections (13.2.1 - 13.2.6) that outline specific conditions and restrictions for permissible transfers. The original text restricts Transfers, while the modified text permits them, creating uncertainty and potential disputes over transfer rights. Sections such as 13.2.3 which outline the 'Change of Ownership Transfer', now clash with the first part of the section stating no transfers are prohibited.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.2.3 Change of Ownership Transfer. Any proposed Transfer that is not described in Subsection 13.2.1 or 13.2,2 is a Change of Ownership Transfer. We will have sixty (60) days from our receipt of the completed and signed franchise application to consent or withhold our consent to any proposed Change of Ownership Transfer. You consent to our communication with any party we deem necessary about the Hotel in order for us to evaluate the proposed Change of Ownership Transfer. Our consent to the Change of Ownership Transfer is subject to the following conditions, all of which must be satisfied at or before the date of closing the Change of Ownership Transfer ("Closing"):
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 13.2 states that transfers of equity interest are not prohibited and can be done as expressly permitted. This directly contradicts section 13.2.3, which outlines specific conditions and requirements for 'Change of Ownership Transfer'. The original text prohibited such transfers unless expressly permitted, setting the stage for the detailed conditions. The changed text removes the prohibition, thereby conflicting with the subsequent conditions in 13.2.3, which assumes a restricted transfer environment. This creates a clear in-text contradiction, fitting the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.
